domains.name: Writing KDNA
domains.description: Editorial writing judgment — diagnose whether content has a real argument, a cognitive hook, and evidence density. Not copy editing.
domains.core_insight: Most writing problems are structural and argument-level, not language-level.

domains.name: Knowledge Management KDNA
domains.description: Knowledge management judgment — distinguish knowledge assets from raw material. A saved note is not knowledge without proposition, boundary, counter-example, and evolution record.
domains.core_insight: Most 'knowledge bases' are raw material collections. Knowledge requires testing, not just saving.

domains.name: Prompt Diagnosis KDNA
domains.description: Prompt diagnosis judgment — identify why a prompt failed (task mixing, goal ambiguity, context gap), not just suggest format improvements.
domains.core_insight: Most prompt failures are task mixing and goal ambiguity, not missing format elements. Fix the cognitive conflict before the formatting.

domains.name: Agent Safety Judgment KDNA
domains.description: Agent safety judgment — halt before irreversible actions, verify authorization, backup, and reversibility. Recommend before executing.
domains.core_insight: Tools make agents capable. Safety judgment makes agents cautious before irreversible action.

domains.name: Open-source Project KDNA
domains.description: Open-source project judgment — diagnose why a project is not gaining adoption. The root cause is usually value-entry clarity, not documentation or feature gaps.
domains.core_insight: Adoption failure is a value-entry problem: visitors cannot determine in 30 seconds what problem this solves and why it is better.

domains.name: Content Strategy KDNA
domains.description: Content strategy judgment — evaluate whether a topic is worth writing based on specific audience, cognitive contrast, discussability, and comprehension barrier. Not topic generation.
domains.core_insight: Ideas are cheap. Worthiness is rare. Filter topics by who they are for and what assumption they challenge.
